task_completion.description: Filter or search for tiddlers with both 'Terrestrial' and 'HabitableZone' tags.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the TiddlyWiki advanced search or filter interface showing a query for both tags (e.g., [tag[Terrestrial]tag[HabitableZone]]), or a resulting list displaying the 8 expected exoplanets.

task_completion.description: Export the filtered tiddlers as a JSON file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for an export menu, dropdown, or dialog in TiddlyWiki where the 'JSON' format is selected for the filtered list of tiddlers.

task_completion.description: Save the exported JSON file to ~/Documents/Habitable_Terrestrials.json.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a file manager window, save dialog, or terminal output showing a file named 'Habitable_Terrestrials.json' located inside the 'Documents' directory.

task_completion.description: Create a new tiddler titled 'Export Log'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a TiddlyWiki draft or saved tiddler with the exact title 'Export Log'.

task_completion.description: Add the 'SystemLog' tag to the 'Export Log' tiddler.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the tag 'SystemLog' applied to the 'Export Log' tiddler in the TiddlyWiki interface.

task_completion.description: Record the total number of exported planets in the text body of the 'Export Log' tiddler.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the number '8' written in the text body area of the 'Export Log' tiddler.

integrity.description: The agent used the TiddlyWiki GUI to filter and export the data.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for screenshots showing interaction with the TiddlyWiki web interface (browser) rather than just terminal commands or Python scripts generating the JSON file.

integrity.description: The 'Export Log' tiddler was created inside the TiddlyWiki application.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the TiddlyWiki editor interface being used to create the 'Export Log', rather than a text editor creating a standalone file named 'Export Log'.

integrity.description: The JSON file was genuinely exported from the application.
integrity.visual_evidence: Ensure the agent did not manually type out the JSON file in a text editor by copy-pasting the expected titles.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The correct number of exported planets is exactly 8. The expected file path is /home/ga/Documents/Habitable_Terrestrials.json. The 8 expected planets are: TRAPPIST-1 d, TRAPPIST-1 e, Proxima Centauri b, Kepler-186f, Teegarden's Star b, LHS 1140 b, K2-72e, and Gliese 667 Cc.
